TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-10: Nancy Hayden's laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: As discussion with nancy hayden over phone , I access her laptop to check and cleanded upto 3.5GB (at login it was space left in 714MB).
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Re: NewStar Remote
TimeEntry.Notes: Initially connected to Jody's system through MyRemote to begin troubleshooting. After encountering connection limitations, attempted to connect again via ManageEngine; however, the session also failed to establish properly. As confirmed by the user The issue will be rechecked once connectivity stabilizes or the user is available for another session.

TimeEntry.Title: Nancy hayden called in for Space issue
TimeEntry.Notes:
Nancy hayden called in for Space issue....

so connect to the user computer and perfomed the clean-up of Temp data and also clean the Software-distribution folder...

Able to increase the 7GB of space and she said she has to leave and will monitor the cleaning process..

hence closing the ticket....

TimeEntry.Title: Backup location [\\10.1.7.11\bkp_BWH] is getting low on free disk space (1.8 TB free of 17.8 TB).
TimeEntry.Notes: Montitor the veeam backups for BWH and found backups were completed with warning alerts due to low space in the backup storage. To resolve this, I cleaned up the old backups that were older than 14 days and then rescanned the repository. After this backup space has increased 1.8 TB to  to 13.9 TB.

TimeEntry.Title: Joanne with BWH on call need assistance as she can't connect remotely
TimeEntry.Notes: Joanne had some issue while connecting from My remote  , She cant see her session Checked the Azure permission and assigned her permission and confirmed able to take remote from MYRemote

TimeEntry.Title: Re: [Huntress Detection] LOW - Incident on BrandywineMyle (BWH - Brandywine Homes)
TimeEntry.Notes: Received a Huntress detection email indicating a password storage alert for MYLE from BWH. I reviewed the alert and composed an email to the user, advising them to properly manage and secure their stored passwords. The message included clear instructions and the file path where the credentials were detected, helping them identify and remove or relocate the sensitive data appropriately.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: [Huntress Detection] LOW - Incident on BrandywineMyle (BWH - Brandywine Homes)
TimeEntry.Notes: Received confirmation via email from BWH that the identified password file for MYLE had been deleted. After verifying the response, I proceeded to remove the corresponding detection from the Huntress portal to close the alert. The incident is now resolved, and no further action is required at this time.

TimeEntry.Title: Joanne Pagliarulo Brandywine Homes- My remote Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Joanne had an issue with the MyRemote site — she was able to access it on her Mac but not from her office computer. I sent her the required software, and after she ran it on her Mac, I was able to connect remotely. The main issue was that she needed to remove access from her office computer but couldn’t access the MyRemote site via the web. I connected to her Mac to assist with setting up MyRemote access. After reviewing the settings, I cleared cookies from Safari, and she was then able to access the site. I also reset her Microsoft 365 password, as she hadn’t removed the old one, and completed the setup. Confirmed that everything is now working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-LT-03 : AI Note Taker Still Here
TimeEntry.Notes:
I’ve made some changes related to the AI note taker settings. Could you please confirm if the desired changes have been successfully applied?

Also dropped an email to get it confirm by client side.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Pon Outlook error and VPN setup
TimeEntry.Notes: Pon intermittently getting redirected to M365 site when navigating Outlook. Investigate the issue and found that he recently changed phones. M365 2fa had conflict with his old/new authenticator apps. Revoked all sessions and recreated MFA to resolve the issue. Re-set Pon's Sophos account. Follow up with Aseel regarding adobe issue and bluebeam activation.

TimeEntry.Title: QNAP firmware 5.3.7.3256 upgrade for BWH
TimeEntry.Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the BWH environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, I verified that all backup jobs had completed successfully. Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalize the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. I then rescanned the backup repository, and the scan completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH Servers are pending reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes: Log in to the Manage Engine to check the servers BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-SQL-01, BWH-HQ-VBR-01, BWH-HQ-VONE, DC03, DC04, FS01 which have not been rebooted. So connected to the servers and rebooted them and verified all the services are running fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Server BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01 pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked  machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the server, and restart the server.
After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machine for installed patches in APD. After scan  the machine is now in healthy systems.
Machine  : BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01

TimeEntry.Title: Servers reboot required:- BWLAP-10
TimeEntry.Notes: While monitoring the Manage Engine Portal, Found the below-mentioned system that needed to reboot, so restarted all the system, After rebootin system has been synced with the portal. hence closing this ticket. Server are:- BWLAP-10

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-LT-05 : Found critical low disk space over here (only 1gb remains)
TimeEntry.Notes: Low disk space detected on BWH-HQ-LT-05. Accessed the machine via backend and performed manual cleanup by removing outdated patch updates and temporary files. Recovered approximately 10 GB of disk space. System storage now within acceptable limits.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Weekly Firewall Backup for BWH FortiGate 100F
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BWH FortiGate 100F Firewall. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Nancy having issue with VPN
TimeEntry.Notes:
ChatGPT said:

Here’s a clean and professional version of your ticket summary you can use in your helpdesk system ??

Ticket Summary

Received a call from user Nancy reporting an issue connecting to the VPN.

Troubleshooting Steps:

  1. Connected to the user’s machine and attempted VPN connection — failed.

  2. Retried the VPN connection — still failed.

  3. Tested using a test user account (BWHvpn10) — same issue persisted.

  4. Rebooted the system and attempted again — no change.

  5. Reset the user’s account in Active Directory — still unable to connect.

  6. Logged into the firewall, removed the registered VPN account, and reconfigured Authenticator.

  7. Tested again with the test account — connection successful.

  8. Contacted Nancy, re-registered her VPN account, and completed Authenticator setup.

  9. Verified VPN connectivity — successful.

Resolution:

VPN connection issue resolved after re-registering user and reconfiguring Authenticator.

Status: Ticket closed.

TimeEntry.Title: User reported continuous virus pop-up notifications appearing on his system.
TimeEntry.Notes:
User: Julio (BWH)
Issue: User reported continuous virus pop-up notifications appearing on his system. Restarting did not resolve the issue.

Actions Taken:
Connected remotely to Julio’s system for investigation. Observed multiple persistent pop-up notifications and partial system freeze impacting usability. Rebooted the system to regain control. Identified and blocked the website responsible for generating the pop-ups. Disabled unwanted browser notifications and cleared cached permissions for that site. Performed a full antivirus scan — no active threats were detected.

Resolution:
All pop-up notifications were successfully removed, and the system is now operating normally. Advised the user to report immediately if similar issues reoccur.

Status: Closed ?

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Agent not synced (The machine is showing offline status on Manage Engine Portal) - BWH-HQ-LT-06
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the alerts on ManageEngine portal for the machine -BWH-HQ-LT-06.the computer is showing as red (offline). Connected to the computer and checked for the updates or patches applied them. Verified the agent configuration, Run health checks on the affected computer to identify this issue,disabled Firewall and Restarted the ManageEngine Services on the affected computers. Sometimes It resolves the issue related to communication between the agent and the ManageEngine server. Apart from all these I repaired the Manege Engine software engine and then the issue was fixed.
TimeEntry.Resource: A. Kumar Poddar CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Agent not synced (The machine is showing offline status on Manage Engine Portal) - BWH-HQ-LT-13
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the alerts on ManageEngine portal for the machine - BWH-HQ-LT-13 .the computer is showing as red (offline). Connected to the computer and checked for the updates or patches applied them. Verified the agent configuration, Run health checks on the affected computer to identify this issue,disabled Firewall and Restarted the ManageEngine Services on the affected computers. Sometimes It resolves the issue related to communication between the agent and the ManageEngine server. Apart from all these I repaired the Manege Engine software engine and then the issue was fixed.
TimeEntry.Resource: A. Kumar Poddar CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: BWH Servers Service Verification and Status Update
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to the BWH servers to verify that the services are running with the local account and updated the status to the team lead

TimeEntry.Title: Server BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-1, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-SQL-01,BWH-HQ-VBR-01, BWH-HQ-VONE, DC03, DC04, FS01 pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked  machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.
After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machine for installed patches in APD. After scan  the machine are now in healthy systems.

Machine : BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-1, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-SQL-01,BWH-HQ-VBR-01, BWH-HQ-VONE, DC03, DC04, FS01

TimeEntry.Title: TABLET-KLFSQ9HN : Low disk space found over this system
TimeEntry.Notes: Initially, only 4 GB of space was available. After accessing the desktop in the backstage and cleaning the temp directory and old patches, I increased the system space to 22 GB.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Weekly Firewall Backup for BWH SOPHOS firewall
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the bwh Sophos firewall. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Agent not synced (The machine is showing offline status on Manage Engine Portal) - BWLAP-06
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the alerts on ManageEngine portal for the machine - BWLAP-06 .the computer is showing as red (offline). Connected to the computer and checked for the updates or patches applied them. Verified the agent configuration, Run health checks on the affected computer to identify this issue,disabled Firewall and Restarted the ManageEngine Services on the affected computers. Sometimes It resolves the issue related to communication between the agent and the ManageEngine server. Apart from all these I repaired the Manege Engine software engine and then the issue was fixed.
TimeEntry.Resource: A. Kumar Poddar CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: CrowdStrike Scans Initiated for Server Security Validation
TimeEntry.Notes:
Description:
Created and initiated CrowdStrike scans for 12 servers covering a total of 21 drives to ensure protection against any suspicious or malicious elements.
These scans are part of proactive security to validate server health and integrity.

Next Steps:
Scans are in progress and will be reviewed tomorrow, as CrowdStrike’s deep scan process typically requires extended completion time for comprehensive analysis.

TimeEntry.Title: FS01 : Manually patch update as APD failed
TimeEntry.Notes:
FS01 : Manually patch update as APD failed

Downloaded the appropriate package from the sites mentioned in MyRMM and installed it successfully. Then rescanned the system, which confirmed the successful installation
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: failed patch updated on machine BWH-HQ-LT-06
TimeEntry.Notes:
After the automatic deployment of patches, I discovered that several high-vulnerability patches had failed on the following machines. To address this, I connected to each affected machine, downloaded the necessary patches, and manually installed them. Following the installations, I rebooted the computers and rescanned the devices through my RMM portal to ensure proper patch application.

Additionally, I verified that all relevant services were functioning correctly post-deployment. It was also noted that some patches could not be deployed due to compatibility issues.

Update for Microsoft WebView2 Runtime (x64) (141.0.3537.99)

Google Chrome (x64) (141.0.7390.122,141.0.7390.123)

Microsoft Edge for chromium business (141.0.3537.92) (x64)
TimeEntry.Resource: A. Kumar Poddar CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: failed patch updated on machine BWH-HQ-DT-04
TimeEntry.Notes:
After the automatic deployment of patches, I discovered that several high-vulnerability patches had failed on the following machines. To address this, I connected to each affected machine, downloaded the necessary patches, and manually installed them. Following the installations, I rebooted the computers and rescanned the devices through my RMM portal to ensure proper patch application.

Additionally, I verified that all relevant services were functioning correctly post-deployment. It was also noted that some patches could not be deployed due to compatibility issues.

Update for Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise Current Channel for x64 2509 of version(19231.20216)

Windows Malicious Software Removal Tool x64 - Oct 2025 (KB890830)

Google Chrome (x64) (141.0.7390.122,141.0.7390.123)
TimeEntry.Resource: A. Kumar Poddar CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Sync Issue– BWH-HQ-SQL-01 & BWLAP-06
TimeEntry.Notes:
Identified a syncing issue in ManageEngine for the devices BWH-HQ-SQL-01 and BWLAP-06. The systems were not properly synchronizing with the ManageEngine server, causing delays in reporting and updates.

Actions Taken:

  • Logged into the ManageEngine portal and verified the sync status for both affected machines.

  • Checked communication between the endpoint agents and the ManageEngine server.

  • Performed necessary troubleshooting steps to re-establish connectivity and resolve synchronization errors.

  • Re-initiated synchronization and confirmed successful communication.

  • Verified in ManageEngine that both devices are now syncing properly and reporting as expected.

Current Status:
The sync issue has been successfully resolved. Both devices (BWH-HQ-SQL-01 and BWLAP-06) are now syncing properly in ManageEngine.

TimeEntry.Title: Compare all BWH devices between Management Engine and MyRemote
TimeEntry.Notes:
Reviewed and compared all BWH devices listed in the Management Engine against those registered in MyRemote.
Identified any devices missing or mismatched between the two systems and verified their operational status.
Added the missing BWH device(s) into MyRemote to ensure full management visibility and consistency.
Validated communication and confirmed all BWH endpoints are now synchronized and properly reporting in both Management Engine and MyRemote.

TimeEntry.Title: CrowdStrike Scans Initiated for Server Security Validation
TimeEntry.Notes:
Reviewed the results of CrowdStrike scans performed on all servers.
Out of 12 servers:

  • 11 servers completed scans successfully.

  • 1 server showed a low-level detection (CuteWriter.exe) which was quarantined by CrowdStrike and subsequently deleted from the portal for full remediation.

  • 3–4 servers did not complete their scans, possibly due to timeout or system interruption. New scans have been initiated on those servers to ensure full coverage and threat validation.

All other systems currently report no detections.

Next Steps:
Monitor the newly initiated scans for completion and review results once available to confirm all systems are clean and compliant.

TimeEntry.Title: Liz - InformXL error
TimeEntry.Notes: InformXL issue caused by Onedrive not syncing. Re-sync onedrive and confirmed that problem is resolved.

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-10: Nancy Hayden's laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Investigate problem with Nancy's PC and freed up over 20GB of Adobe updates.

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-10: Nancy Hayden's laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Prepare replacement laptop for Nancy.

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-10: Nancy Hayden's laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Stop by main office to pick up power supply for replacement laptop. Set up laptop for Nancy but found that she needed access to Sales DB in Newstar. Performed some troubleshooting on her old laptop and found root cause of memory issue to be that the pagefile was set to a fixed amount instead of system managed. Updated the setting and removed unused mailbox from Outlook to free up space. Confirmed that this fixed her issue. Replacement laptop will remain onsite for a few weeks in case this problem returns. Help Dave Rea with his Onedrive sync. He does not use that laptop all the time and his onedrive was out of date. Help Aseel with Bluebeam credentials recovery and Adobe Acrobat issue with combining docs. Check out Traci's slow PC and recommended to Dave that she get a new computer.

TimeEntry.Title: Tools on BWH-HQ-DT-04
TimeEntry.Notes: As per Sunny’s email, connected to BWH-HQ-DT-04, downloaded and installed our technician tools, and updated Windows with the latest security patches. The status was updated to Sunny after completion

TimeEntry.Title: Installed the MyDisk agent on all BWH servers
TimeEntry.Notes: Reinstalled the MyDisk agent on the server FS-01 and verified that it is now successfully communicating with the portal.

TimeEntry.Title: ACH software installation
TimeEntry.Notes: Call with Newstar team and Pon to discuss ACH software installation.

TimeEntry.Title: VPN MFA Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Fixed an issue with MFA on BWH Firewall relates to timeout on the texchanged toiken.

TimeEntry.Title: Software installation for EFTM for ACH/EFT payment
TimeEntry.Notes: Exchanged emails with Tayler and Pon in attempt to get installation software for ACH/EFT application.

TimeEntry.Title: VPN timeout issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Some users kept getting their accounts locked with attempting to access VPN. After extensive troubleshooting, found that there was an offset of about 15-20 seconds where the MFA code would expire too early. Asked Hamid to adjust that value and verified that MFA is functioning and no longer causing account lockouts.

TimeEntry.Title: Applied a failed patch upgrade on Meraki
TimeEntry.Notes: Applied a jfailed patch deployment for MEraki APs and verified the status of the deployment.

TimeEntry.Title: Pentest reports-Recommendation applied to SFOS
TimeEntry.Notes: Applied the recommanded port closure by pentest to the SFOS firewall and verified network protection status.

TimeEntry.Title: Pon - onedrive issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Cleared temp files. Updated registryreg add "HKCU\Software\Microsoft\OneDrive" /v FilesOnDemandEnabled /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f . Ran SFC & DISM scans.

TimeEntry.Title: Traci - PC setup
TimeEntry.Notes: New PC setup. OS upgrade to Pro. Drivers, patches, apps installation.

TimeEntry.Title: Traci - PC setup
TimeEntry.Notes: Drop off PC to BWH main office. Join to domain and ensure PC is accessible remotely for further configuration. At office, installed newstar and finish PC configuration and data transfer.

TimeEntry.Title: Traci - PC setup
TimeEntry.Notes: Set up Traci's PC and ensure all data/apps are transferred. Set up plotter for sandy and julio.

TimeEntry.Title: Traci - Newstar Sales (Live & Proto databases) not working for Traci
TimeEntry.Notes: Troubleshoot Newstar issue with Live and Proto database. Reviewed Newstar installation docs and updated Traci's configuration. Verified that she can now access both databases.

TimeEntry.Title: Check for missing Anthem emails sent to ap@brandywine-homes.com
TimeEntry.Notes: Check out message trace for missing emails. Generated a report from July 31 to present. Waiting for report to compile then will analyze for missing emails.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam 365 backups were running from last 2 days for BWH
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Veeam 365 server TE-DC-BK-365-01 and found backups were running from last 2 days for BWH and were stuck . Tried to stop the job and start the job. But nothing happened. Then open the case with Veeam 365 support team. After the troubleshooting suggested by support, backups are passed now.

TimeEntry.Title: Check for missing Anthem emails sent to ap@brandywine-homes.com
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed email log but did not find anything for Anthem. Also checked INKY logs and did not find any emails. Sent log csv file to Pon for review and updated him on the results. He believes that Anthem did not actually send the emails.

TimeEntry.Title: Chris Ricoh stapling not working
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into Ricoh and verified that Stapling module is installed. Updated driver to latest version and waited for system patches to apply. Updated setting for stapling and verified that it is now functioning.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-LT-03: Printer Issues
TimeEntry.Notes:
The user had inadvertently deleted their printer and was unsure which one it was. Upon diagnosis, no USB printer was found connected—only two LAN printers of the same model were available. Both LAN printers were reinstalled successfully.

Additionally, the staple feature was reviewed during troubleshooting. The option was visible but greyed out, indicating it was disabled or not properly configured. The issue has been transferred to Parveen and Sunny for further assistance and resolution.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Help with Vendor emails
TimeEntry.Notes:
User Rosa reported that she is not getting email from Laarni Reyes.....

so Login to the office 365 portal and trace the email found that emails are showing as quarantine ..

So release the mails and also found that the domain is added in blocklist....

so removed the domain and add the domain to whitelist...

user confirmed she has received the mails now....
